title: Class Definition
text: Which keyword is used to define a class in Python?
explanation: Python uses the 'class' keyword to define classes. 'def' is for functions. 'struct' is not a Python keyword. 'object' is a built-in base class.

title: Self Parameter
text: In Python instance methods, what does the 'self' parameter represent?
explanation: 'self' refers to the current instance of the class. It is passed implicitly by Python when calling instance methods. Unlike 'this' in Java/C++, self must be explicitly listed as the first parameter.

title: Inheritance
text: What is the correct syntax for class B to inherit from class A?
explanation: Python uses parentheses for inheritance: class Child(Parent). Multiple inheritance: class Child(Parent1, Parent2).

title: Dunder Methods
text: Which method is called when len(obj) is executed?
explanation: len(obj) internally calls obj.__len__(). This is the Pythonic way to define the length of custom objects.

title: @staticmethod vs @classmethod
text: What is the key difference between @staticmethod and @classmethod?

options.text: @classmethod is used only for abstract classes

explanation: @classmethod receives the class as the first parameter (cls) and can access/modify class state. @staticmethod receives no special first parameter and behaves like a regular function.

title: Encapsulation
text: How do you indicate a 'private' attribute in Python?
explanation: Double underscore prefix triggers name mangling (_ClassName__attr), making it harder to access from outside. Single underscore is a convention for 'protected', not enforced by Python.

title: Property Decorator
text: What does @property do in a Python class?

options.text: Makes a method accessible as an attribute

explanation: @property allows a method to be accessed like an attribute (without parentheses). It enables getter/setter patterns with controlled access.

title: Abstract Base Class
text: Which module provides abstract base class support in Python?
explanation: The 'abc' module (Abstract Base Classes) provides ABC and abstractmethod decorator. A class inheriting from ABC with abstract methods cannot be instantiated.

title: Multiple Inheritance - MRO
text: What determines the method resolution order (MRO) in Python?
explanation: Python uses C3 linearization for MRO: child first, then parents in order of definition, but ensuring a consistent ordering (linearization). You can check MRO with ClassName.__mro__.

title: Slots
text: What does __slots__ do in a Python class?

options.text: Restricts attribute creation to listed names
Creates static methods for the class

explanation: __slots__ restricts a class to only the listed attribute names and prevents the creation of __dict__. This saves memory when many instances are created but prevents dynamic attribute assignment.
